title: Underneath Midnight Cravings Moroccan Tagine
description: A grassy, tender Moroccan dish that satisfies midnight cravings, featuring a blend of earthy spices and fresh herbs, all slow-cooked in a wooden-board vessel.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with salt, black pepper, cumin, coriander, and paprika.
Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and ginger and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the butternut squash and sweet potatoes and cook, stirring occasionally, until slightly tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken thighs and cook, stirring occasionally, until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the water and bring to a boil.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and cook, covered, until the chicken is cooked through and the vegetables are tender, about 45 minutes.
Stir in the lemon juice and honey.
Garnish with parsley and cilantro and serve hot.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired.
Serve with couscous or rice, if desired.
